title: cobaea lutea f. viorna
intro: Cobaea lutea f. viorna, also known as the 'Golden Cup' or 'Mexican Cupflower', is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Bignoniaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Cobaea lutea f. viorna are typically dried and made into teas or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
